items_library.name: Lantern & Blade — Starter Items
items_library.author: Lantern & Blade Project
items_library.description: A spare but well-tuned starter library: mundane arms and armor, a handful of consumables, and a small set of old magics. Weighted for a party of one at low levels.

steel_longsword.name: Steel Longsword
steel_longsword.description: A plain soldier's blade, the kind a small-town armorer turns out by the pair. The grip is leather over wood; the crossguard is honest iron. No provenance, no runes, nothing to steal.

shortbow.name: Shortbow
shortbow.description: A plain hunter's bow of layered yew, string waxed against damp. Accurate out to the length of a corridor; unkind at close quarters.

silver_dagger.name: Silver-Edged Dagger
silver_dagger.description: A stout fighting knife, the leading edge and point fitted with a thin ribbon of silver along the steel. The silver is soft and dulls quickly, but it bites things that honest steel will not.

magic.special_effects: The silvered edge cuts through resistances that spare ordinary steel — lycanthropes, lesser undead, fey flesh. GM may waive damage resistance to non-magical weapons for these kinds of foes.

warhammer.name: Warhammer
warhammer.description: A square-headed smith's hammer reshaped for less patient work. Heavy enough to break a helmet, small enough to swing in a crypt.

quarterstaff.name: Iron-Shod Quarterstaff
quarterstaff.description: Six feet of seasoned oak, capped at either end with a band of dull iron. Reach, balance, and the quiet assumption that it will outlast several of its wielder's weapons.

lanternblade.name: Lanternblade
lanternblade.description: A longsword whose fuller is inlaid with a single unbroken line of pale runes. In low light the runes warm visibly; in true dark the blade sheds a steady lantern-glow as far as a thrown stone. Old Warden work, from before the Watch broke.

magic.special_effects: Sheds dim lantern-light in a short radius whenever drawn. The light cannot be extinguished while the blade is bare. Undead struck by the blade flinch visibly, even on a glancing hit.

leather_armor.name: Boiled Leather
leather_armor.description: Hardened leather over a quilted undergarment. Quiet, flexible, and a great deal lighter than its owner usually wishes it were when something hits them.

chain_mail.name: Chain Mail
chain_mail.description: Thousands of small riveted rings over a padded gambeson. Heavy, patient, and noisy — chain mail is the sound of a soldier who has made up their mind.

magic.special_effects: Stealth attempts while wearing chain mail are made with disadvantage. The rings rattle at every movement.

iron_shield.name: Iron-Banded Shield
iron_shield.description: A round shield of planked oak bound with iron. Scarred, dented, and still shield-shaped. That's the test.

ring_of_protection.name: Ring of the Warding Hand
ring_of_protection.description: A band of twisted silver wire set with a cloudy moonstone. The stone cools against the wearer's skin when harm is near — a small, useful warning.

magic.special_effects: The wearer also gains a +1 bonus to all saving throws. The bonus stacks with other magical protections.

cloak_of_quiet_tread.name: Cloak of Quiet Tread
cloak_of_quiet_tread.description: A long traveler's cloak of soft grey wool. Its hem never quite catches on doorframes; its folds never quite make the sound they should. Hard to notice. That is the point.

magic.special_effects: The wearer gains advantage on Stealth checks to move quietly. The cloak does not help against detection by sight, smell, or magical sense — only sound.

amulet_of_the_salamander.name: Amulet of the Salamander
amulet_of_the_salamander.description: A small lozenge of black glass on a braided leather cord, veined with a single thread of red that seems to pulse in firelight. Warm to the touch, always.

magic.special_effects: The wearer is unburned by ordinary flame — torches, campfires, a thrown oil flask — and takes half damage from magical fire.

gauntlets_of_the_quarryman.name: Gauntlets of the Quarryman
gauntlets_of_the_quarryman.description: Thick leather gauntlets stitched with flat iron plates across the back of each hand. The leather has the pale dust of old limestone worked deep into the grain — the smell of stonecutting never quite comes out.

magic.special_effects: The wearer's grip is unnaturally sure. They have advantage on Athletics checks to lift, shove, or grapple, and their hands do not slip on wet or bloody hafts.

healing_potion.name: Healing Draught
healing_potion.description: A thumb-sized glass vial of something dark red and honey-thick. It tastes unapologetically of iron and pine resin and heals what it can reach.

antitoxin.name: Flask of Antitoxin
antitoxin.description: A narrow flask of cloudy grey-green liquid stoppered with wax. Bitter beyond description. Worth every wince.

consumable.amount: Removes the poisoned condition, or grants advantage on the next save against a poison effect if consumed before exposure.

scroll_of_mending.name: Scroll of Mending
scroll_of_mending.description: A palm-sized parchment roll sealed with a bead of green wax. The sigil is a chain's closed link.

consumable.amount: On use, the scroll repairs one small break — a snapped chain, a cracked shield-boss, a torn map, a shattered vial. The scroll is consumed regardless of the size of the target; oversized objects remain unmended.

magic.special_effects: The repair is physically perfect but not magical restoration — a mended magical item that was broken beyond its enchantment remains mundane.

wand_of_sparks.name: Wand of Sparks
wand_of_sparks.description: A finger-length rod of polished bone, bound at both ends with fine copper wire. When gripped, the wire tingles faintly. In dark places it smells very softly of ozone.

magic.charge_effect: Spend 1 charge to unleash a crackling arc at a target within short range: ranged spell attack, 2d6 lightning damage on a hit, half on a miss due to splash. On a natural 1 on the attack roll, the wand sparks wildly and the wielder takes 1d4 lightning damage.
magic.special_effects: The wand can be used as a plain short club when out of charges; the bone is hard and the copper bites.

torch.name: Pitch-Soaked Torch
torch.description: A length of oak wrapped at one end with rags and pitch. Burns bright for about an hour; the grip gets warm, the smell gets into everything.

rope_50ft.name: Hempen Rope (50 ft)
rope_50ft.description: Fifty feet of tarred hemp rope, coiled and slung. Heavier than you remember. Useful more often than you expect.

rubbing_of_the_lantern_tree.name: Charcoal Rubbing of the Lantern Tree
rubbing_of_the_lantern_tree.description: A folded sheet of soft paper, roughly the size of a tabard, carrying a charcoal rubbing taken from the base of some very old standing stone. The image is of a branching tree whose fruits are hanging lanterns, and beneath it a line of worn script: 'WE KEEP THE LIGHT SO THE LIGHT KEEPS US.' The motto of the Wardens of the Watch — and the mark that, whatever else a Lanternblade is, it is theirs.
